response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of an open-source GIS/geo-data platform, web GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and has real low-latency systems experience from Sociomantic, where he built high-throughput asynchronous services in DLang. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his only C/C++ industry experience is a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago; his recent work (Stripe, Shopify, RIDE) is TypeScript/Node full-stack product engineering. He also has no direct routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance pipeline experience, making him a lateral rather than direct fit.
strengths: Extensive geospatial/GIS background, including founding and operating an open-source geo-data mapping platform
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, caching, fibers) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling, and Linux/Kubernetes operational experience
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2014-2015); no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles (2020-present) are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, far from systems-level data pipeline engineering

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C++/Rust systems work
